Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Understanding the Go Runtime: The Scheduler 84 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Vite 8.0 Is Out 132 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. The Met releases high-def 3D scans of 140 famous art objects 263 upvotes, 50 comments
  4. WolfIP: Lightweight TCP/IP stack with no dynamic memory allocations 119 upvotes, 19 comments
  5. IMG_0416 (2024) 56 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. DDR4 Sdram – Initialization, Training and Calibration 90 upvotes, 19 comments
  7. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 309 upvotes, 94 comments
  8. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 1176 upvotes, 429 comments
  9. Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust 139 upvotes, 41 comments
  10. Shall I implement it? No 1144 upvotes, 433 comments
  11. Long overlooked as crucial to life, fungi start to get their due 123 upvotes, 39 comments
  12. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 286 upvotes, 113 comments
  13. Willingness to look stupid 164 upvotes, 62 comments
  14. Document poisoning in RAG systems: How attackers corrupt AI's sources 107 upvotes, 40 comments
  15. Celebrating Interesting Flickr Technologies 20 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. Innocent woman jailed after being misidentified using AI facial recognition 549 upvotes, 283 comments
  17. “This is not the computer for you” 257 upvotes, 124 comments
  18. Prefix sums at gigabytes per second with ARM NEON 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  19. Lost Doctor Who Episodes Found 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  20. Launch HN: IonRouter (YC W26) – High-throughput, low-cost inference 61 upvotes, 24 comments
  21. Bringing Chrome to ARM64 Linux Devices 87 upvotes, 44 comments
  22. Show HN: Axe – A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework 175 upvotes, 100 comments
  23. Hyperlinks in Terminal Emulators 25 upvotes, 9 comments
  24. Big data on the cheapest MacBook 340 upvotes, 273 comments
  25. Are LLM merge rates not getting better? 137 upvotes, 121 comments
  26. ATMs didn’t kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 388 upvotes, 416 comments
  27. Show HN: Global Maritime Chokepoints 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. US private credit defaults hit record 9.2% in 2025, Fitch says 334 upvotes, 397 comments
  29. Grief and the AI split 121 upvotes, 174 comments
  30. Can you instruct a robot to make a PBJ sandwich? 23 upvotes, 26 comments

Last generated 3:00pm, 13 March 2026. Source here.
